questions.question: What is the rank of India in ‘Liberal Democracy Index’ of V-Dem Democracy Report 2022
questions.explanation: Sweden-based V-Dem Institute (Varieties of Democracy research project) released a report, titled ‘Democracy Report 2022: Autocratization Changing Nature?’. India ranked 93 in the Liberal Democracy Index, and 100 in the Electoral Democracy Index. India has figured in the top 10 autocratising countries of the world along with El Salvador, Turkey and Hungary.

questions.question: ‘National Vaccination Day’ is observed in India to commemorate the first dose of which vaccine administered in India?
questions.explanation: National Vaccination Day is observed annually on 16 March across India. The occasion is celebrated to raise awareness about importance of vaccination. India administered its first dose of oral vaccine against Polio on this day in 1995. It marked the launch of National Immunization Day (NID), also known as National Vaccination Day and Pulse Polio Immunisation programme.

questions.question: Which day has been declared by the UN as ‘International Day to Combat Islamophobia’?
questions.explanation: The U.N. General Assembly approved a resolution setting March 15 as the International Day to Combat Islamophobia. The resolution was adopted by consensus by the UN and cosponsored by 55 mainly Muslim countries. It emphasizes the right to freedom of religion and belief and recalls a 1981 resolution calling for “the elimination of all forms of intolerance and of discrimination based on religion or belief.”

questions.question: Where was the India-Japan Annual Summit 2022 held?
questions.explanation: Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and his Japanese counterpart Fumio Kishida held talks in New Delhi during the India-Japan Annual Summit 2022. After the Meet, the Indian Prime Minister announced that Japan is set to invest 5 trillion yen or Rs 3.2 lakh crore in India over next 5 years. This is Japan PM’s first visit to India as the head of the Japanese government.

questions.question: Which gas was associated with the Bhopal disaster or the Bhopal gas tragedy?
questions.explanation: The Bhopal gas tragedy, was a gas leak accident in 1984 at the Union Carbide India Limited (UCIL) pesticide plant in Bhopal. Over 5 lakh people were exposed to methyl isocyanate (MIC) gas. Around 337 tonnes of hazardous waste at the Union Carbide’s plant in Bhopal are set to be burnt at an incinerator at Pithampur industrial areas in Indore, 37 years after the gas leak incident.

questions.question: Zojila Pass, connects which Indian state/UT with the rest of the country?
questions.explanation: Zojila Pass provides link between Kashmir Valley and Ladakh on Srinagar-Kargil-Leh Road, which connects Ladakh to the rest of the country. The strategic pass has been opened by the Border Road Organisation (BRO). It is located at an altitude of 11,650 feet. The pass normally closes by end-November every year and opened around mid-April the following year.

questions.question: The amendments to the ‘Limited Liability Partnership’ 2008 will take effect from which date?
questions.explanation: The amendments to the ‘Limited Liability Partnership’ 2008 will take effect from April 1. The Amendment has introduced the concept of ‘Small Limited Liability Partnership’. The LLPs are those with a previous year turnover of up to ₹40 lakh or any prescribed amount which shall not exceed ₹50 crore. The Government has the powers to designate certain LLPs as ‘start-up LLPs’.

questions.question: Germany has signed the Energy Deal with which North-Eastern country, to reduce its dependence on Russia?
questions.explanation: Germany has signed the Energy Deal with Qatar as a long-term energy partnership. Russia has been the largest supplier of gas to Germany. Germany, Europe’s biggest economy is seeking to become less dependent on Russian energy sources, after Russia started to invade Ukraine. Qatar’s Emir and Germany’s Minister also discussed ways to enhance bilateral relations in the energy sector.

questions.question: The Land Ports Authority of India (LPAI) is a statutory body working under which Union Ministry?
questions.explanation: The Land Ports Authority of India (LPAI) is a statutory body working under Ministry of Home Affairs. It was created through the Land Ports Authority of India Act, 2010. LPAI is responsible for creating, maintaining and managing border infrastructure in India. The 10th anniversary of the Land Port Authority (LPA) was recently organised. India’s 15,000 km-long land borders with Afghanistan, China, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, Myanmar and Pakistan.

questions.question: In which city, was the “Step-Up to End TB- World TB Day Summit 2022” organised?
questions.explanation: Union Health Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya inaugurated the “Step-Up to End TB- World TB Day Summit” on the occasion of World Tuberculosis Day at New Delhi. Union Health Minister will award well-performing states and districts for achieving TB elimination. In 2018, Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired the Delhi End TB summit and proposed to End TB in India by 2025, five years ahead of the TB-related SDG targets of 2030.
